queue.title: The Standalone Substrate: Why I Extracted Hermes
queue.hook: I built the Hermes memory substrate for myself, then I realized I was gatekeeping the very thing I wanted to democratize.
queue.notes: Published to LinkedIn via Blotato on June 1, 2026. Narrative post about modular Hermes extraction.

queue.title: How to Join the Revolt: The Road4AI Contributor Guide
queue.hook: Stop renting your intelligence. Start building the systems that run it.
queue.notes: Thread highlighting the new CONTRIBUTING.md and how to get involved.

queue.title: My Agent has Alzheimer's
queue.hook: To be honest, it is like talking to a stranger with Alzheimer's.
queue.notes: Hits the core realization that conversational memory != execution memory.

queue.title: Memory Needs an Expiration Date
queue.hook: You have not built memory. You have built a hoard.
queue.notes: Published to X via Blotato on June 1, 2026. Thread adaptation on memory governance; all posts approved under X constraints.

queue.title: Memory Needs an Expiration Date
queue.hook: If your agent remembers everything, it will eventually understand nothing.
queue.notes: Published to LinkedIn via Blotato on June 1, 2026. Tutorial on memory governance and forgetting on purpose.

queue.title: The Self-Optimizing Agent: End of Prompt Engineering
queue.hook: I stopped 'prompt engineering' and started building agents that fix themselves. This is how the system actually learns from its own mistakes.

queue.title: Why I Still Use a Physical Notebook
queue.hook: I built a 5-agent pipeline to automate my content, then I picked up a pen and started writing in a physical journal. Here’s why optimization is a trap.

queue.title: Escaping the 12-Month Agent Lag
queue.hook: If you’re still 'prompt engineering' a single agent, you’re building a legacy system. The 2026 stack is about stateless swarm coordination.

queue.title: The Swarm That Deployed Itself
queue.hook: I just watched a team of 5 AI agents deploy themselves, update their own operating contracts, and then offer to QA their own work unprompted.

queue.title: The Struggle of Scale: When HNSW Breaks
queue.hook: Local HNSW is a lie once you hit 100+ agents. I thought I had the memory substrate solved until I tried to scale the swarm.

queue.title: From 0% to 100%: The Triage Win
queue.hook: Last week, my incident triage agent was hallucinating 40% of the root causes. This morning, it hit a 100% pass rate on the evaluation suite.

queue.title: The Emergence Moment
queue.hook: There is a specific second when 'I told it to' becomes 'it understood.'

queue.title: Production RAG vs. Toys
queue.hook: Forget demo-tier RAG. If you're not using distributed vector storage for your enterprise data, you're just playing with toys.

queue.title: Hermes v2.0: The Self-Indexing Reveal (Honest Teaser)
queue.hook: I didn't index the internet. I indexed myself.

queue.title: The Plate Realization (Struggle)
queue.hook: To be honest, I asked my agent what was on its plate and it gave me the most terrifying answer possible.

queue.title: The Autonomy Trap
queue.hook: I almost shipped a Self-Healing Constitution.

queue.title: The 181ms Honesty Call
queue.hook: I saw a demo yesterday claiming 10ms latency for a local agent query.

queue.title: The Security Theater Trap
queue.hook: I almost shipped a security layer that was 100% theater.
queue.notes: Refactored to abstract exploit details while keeping the failure receipt.
